item.title: FileGram: Grounding Agent Personalization in File-System Behavioral Traces
item.description: Coworking AI agents operating within local file systems are rapidly emerging as a paradigm in human-AI interaction; however, effective personalization remains limited by severe data constraints, as strict privacy barriers and the difficu...

item.title: BidirLM: From Text to Omnimodal Bidirectional Encoders by Adapting and Composing Causal LLMs
item.description: Transforming causal generative language models into bidirectional encoders offers a powerful alternative to BERT-style architectures. However, current approaches remain limited: they lack consensus on optimal training objectives, suffer...

item.title: Adam's Law: Textual Frequency Law on Large Language Models
item.description: While textual frequency has been validated as relevant to human cognition in reading speed, its relatedness to Large Language Models (LLMs) is seldom studied. We propose a novel research direction in terms of textual data frequency, whic...

item.title: AURA: Always-On Understanding and Real-Time Assistance via Video Streams
item.description: Video Large Language Models (VideoLLMs) have achieved strong performance on many video understanding tasks, but most existing systems remain offline and are not well-suited for live video streams that require continuous observation and t...

item.title: SkillX: Automatically Constructing Skill Knowledge Bases for Agents
item.description: Learning from experience is critical for building capable large language model (LLM) agents, yet prevailing self-evolving paradigms remain inefficient: agents learn in isolation, repeatedly rediscover similar behaviors from limited exper...

item.title: Can LLMs Learn to Reason Robustly under Noisy Supervision?
item.description: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) effectively trains reasoning models that rely on abundant perfect labels, but its vulnerability to unavoidable noisy labels due to expert scarcity remains critically underexplored. In...

item.title: CLEAR: Unlocking Generative Potential for Degraded Image Understanding in Unified Multimodal Models
item.description: Image degradation from blur, noise, compression, and poor illumination severely undermines multimodal understanding in real-world settings. Unified multimodal models that combine understanding and generation within a single architecture...

item.title: Mimic Intent, Not Just Trajectories
item.description: While imitation learning (IL) has achieved impressive success in dexterous manipulation through generative modeling and pretraining, state-of-the-art approaches like Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models still struggle with adaptation to e...

item.title: Do Audio-Visual Large Language Models Really See and Hear?
item.description: Audio-Visual Large Language Models (AVLLMs) are emerging as unified interfaces to multimodal perception. We present the first mechanistic interpretability study of AVLLMs, analyzing how audio and visual features evolve and fuse through d...

item.title: Type-Checked Compliance: Deterministic Guardrails for Agentic Financial Systems Using Lean 4 Theorem Proving
item.description: The rapid evolution of autonomous, agentic artificial intelligence within financial services has introduced an existential architectural crisis: large language models (LLMs) are probabilistic, non-deterministic systems operating in domai...

item.title: Locally Confident, Globally Stuck: The Quality-Exploration Dilemma in Diffusion Language Models
item.description: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) theoretically permit token decoding in arbitrary order, a flexibility that could enable richer exploration of reasoning paths than autoregressive (AR) LLMs. In practice, however, random-order decod...

item.title: TriAttention: Efficient Long Reasoning with Trigonometric KV Compression
item.description: Extended reasoning in large language models (LLMs) creates severe KV cache memory bottlenecks. Leading KV cache compression methods estimate KV importance using attention scores from recent post-RoPE queries. However, queries rotate with...

item.title: LIBERO-Para: A Diagnostic Benchmark and Metrics for Paraphrase Robustness in VLA Models
item.description: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models achieve strong performance in robotic manipulation by leveraging pre-trained vision-language backbones. However, in downstream robotic settings, they are typically fine-tuned with limited data, leading...

item.title: Memory Intelligence Agent
item.description: Deep research agents (DRAs) integrate LLM reasoning with external tools. Memory systems enable DRAs to leverage historical experiences, which are essential for efficient reasoning and autonomous evolution. Existing methods rely on retrie...

item.title: ClawArena: Benchmarking AI Agents in Evolving Information Environments
item.description: AI agents deployed as persistent assistants must maintain correct beliefs as their information environment evolves. In practice, evidence is scattered across heterogeneous sources that often contradict one another, new information can in...

item.title: LightThinker++: From Reasoning Compression to Memory Management
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) excel at complex reasoning, yet their efficiency is limited by the surging cognitive overhead of long thought traces. In this paper, we propose LightThinker, a method that enables LLMs to dynamically compress...

item.title: Unifying Group-Relative and Self-Distillation Policy Optimization via Sample Routing
item.description: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a standard paradigm for post-training large language models. While Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is widely adopted, its coarse credit assignment uniformly penal...

item.title: Vero: An Open RL Recipe for General Visual Reasoning
item.description: What does it take to build a visual reasoner that works across charts, science, spatial understanding, and open-ended tasks? The strongest vision-language models (VLMs) show such broad visual reasoning is within reach, but the recipe beh...

item.title: PLUME: Latent Reasoning Based Universal Multimodal Embedding
item.description: Universal multimodal embedding (UME) maps heterogeneous inputs into a shared retrieval space with a single model. Recent approaches improve UME by generating explicit chain-of-thought (CoT) rationales before extracting embeddings, enabli...

item.title: ONE-SHOT: Compositional Human-Environment Video Synthesis via Spatial-Decoupled Motion Injection and Hybrid Context Integration
item.description: Recent advances in Video Foundation Models (VFMs) have revolutionized human-centric video synthesis, yet fine-grained and independent editing of subjects and scenes remains a critical challenge. Recent attempts to incorporate richer envi...

item.title: Paper Espresso: From Paper Overload to Research Insight
item.description: The accelerating pace of scientific publishing makes it increasingly difficult for researchers to stay current. We present Paper Espresso, an open-source platform that automatically discovers, summarizes, and analyzes trending arXiv pape...

item.title: The Geometric Alignment Tax: Tokenization vs. Continuous Geometry in Scientific Foundation Models
item.description: Foundation models for biology and physics optimize predictive accuracy, but their internal representations systematically fail to preserve the continuous geometry of the systems they model. We identify the root cause: the Geometric Align...

item.title: Cog-DRIFT: Exploration on Adaptively Reformulated Instances Enables Learning from Hard Reasoning Problems
item.description: Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) has improved the reasoning abilities of LLMs, yet a fundamental limitation remains: models cannot learn from problems that are too difficult to solve under their current policy, as th...

item.title: Scaling Teams or Scaling Time? Memory Enabled Lifelong Learning in LLM Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: Large language model (LLM) multi-agent systems can scale along two distinct dimensions: by increasing the number of agents and by improving through accumulated experience over time. Although prior work has studied these dimensions separa...

item.title: POEMetric: The Last Stanza of Humanity
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) can compose poetry, but how far are they from human poets? In this paper, we introduce POEMetric, the first comprehensive framework for poetry evaluation, examining 1) basic instruction-following abilities in...

item.title: MinerU2.5-Pro: Pushing the Limits of Data-Centric Document Parsing at Scale
item.description: Current document parsing methods compete primarily on model architecture innovation, while systematic engineering of training data remains underexplored. Yet SOTA models of different architectures and parameter scales exhibit highly cons...

item.title: Self-Execution Simulation Improves Coding Models
item.description: A promising research direction in enabling LLMs to generate consistently correct code involves addressing their inability to properly estimate program execution, particularly for code they generate. In this work, we demonstrate that Code...

item.title: Your Agent, Their Asset: A Real-World Safety Analysis of OpenClaw
item.description: OpenClaw, the most widely deployed personal AI agent in early 2026, operates with full local system access and integrates with sensitive services such as Gmail, Stripe, and the filesystem. While these broad privileges enable high levels...

item.title: Learning to Learn-at-Test-Time: Language Agents with Learnable Adaptation Policies
item.description: Test-Time Learning (TTL) enables language agents to iteratively refine their performance through repeated interactions with the environment at inference time. At the core of TTL is an adaptation policy that updates the actor policy based...

item.title: Less Detail, Better Answers: Degradation-Driven Prompting for VQA
item.description: Recent advancements in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have significantly pushed the boundaries of Visual Question Answering (VQA).However,high-resolution details can sometimes become noise that leads to hallucinations or reasoning errors....

item.title: Synthetic Sandbox for Training Machine Learning Engineering Agents
item.description: As large language model agents advance beyond software engineering (SWE) tasks toward machine learning engineering (MLE), verifying agent behavior becomes orders of magnitude more expensive: while SWE tasks can be verified via fast-execu...

item.title: Emergent Compositional Communication for Latent World Properties
item.description: Can multi-agent communication pressure extract discrete, compositional representations of invisible physical properties from frozen video features? We show that agents communicating through a Gumbel-Softmax bottleneck with iterated learn...

item.title: SpatialEdit: Benchmarking Fine-Grained Image Spatial Editing
item.description: Image spatial editing performs geometry-driven transformations, allowing precise control over object layout and camera viewpoints. Current models are insufficient for fine-grained spatial manipulations, motivating a dedicated assessment...

item.title: HDP: A Lightweight Cryptographic Protocol for Human Delegation Provenance in Agentic AI Systems
item.description: Agentic AI systems increasingly execute consequential actions on behalf of human principals, delegating tasks through multi-step chains of autonomous agents. No existing standard addresses a fundamental accountability gap: verifying that...

item.title: SciLT: Long-Tailed Classification in Scientific Image Domains
item.description: Long-tailed recognition has benefited from foundation models and fine-tuning paradigms, yet existing studies and benchmarks are mainly confined to natural image domains, where pre-training and fine-tuning data share similar distributions...

item.title: OpenWorldLib: A Unified Codebase and Definition of Advanced World Models
item.description: World models have garnered significant attention as a promising research direction in artificial intelligence, yet a clear and unified definition remains lacking. In this paper, we introduce OpenWorldLib, a comprehensive and standardized...

item.title: AvatarPointillist: AutoRegressive 4D Gaussian Avatarization
item.description: We introduce AvatarPointillist, a novel framework for generating dynamic 4D Gaussian avatars from a single portrait image. At the core of our method is a decoder-only Transformer that autoregressively generates a point cloud for 3D Gauss...
